questions.question: A card is drawn from an ordinary pack and gambler bets that it is a spade or an ace. What is the probability of his winning the bet?
questions.explanation: Let A: a spade is drawn and B: an ace is drawn Probability of winning the bet = P (A or B) = P (A) + P (B) - P (A and B) = 13/52 +4/52 - 1/52 = 16/52 = 4/13

questions.question: Find the value of (x³ - 1/x³) if (x+ 1/x) = 7.

Q and R together can complete a piece of work in 8 days, P and Q together can complete the same work in 12 days, and P and R together can complete the same work in 16 days. In, how many days can P, Q and R together completes the same work?
questions.explanation: Q and R together can complete a work in = ⅛ P and Q together can complete a work in = 1/12 P and R together can complete a work in = 1/16 Work completed by 2(P+ Q + R) in a day = ⅛+1/12+1/16 = (6+4+3)/48=13/48 Work complete by (P + Q + R) in a day = 13/(48 X 2)=13/96 So, P, Q and R together can complete the work in 96/13 days.

questions.question: Find the value of (p² - q²⁾/r + (q² - r²⁾/p + (r² - p²⁾ /q ; if (p + q + r) = 0
If a²+b² +ab = 40 and a² + b² - ab= 10, find the value of a⁴+ b⁴+a ²b²
questions.explanation: => a ² + b² + ab = 40 --------① => a ² + b² - ab - 10 --------② => ① * ② => (a ²+ b²)² - (ab)² = 40 * 10 => a⁴ + b⁴ + 2a² b² - a² b² = 400 => a⁴ + b⁴ + a²b² = 400

questions.question: Ramesh bought 200 watches for Rs. 150 per watch. He spent Rs. 10000 for transportation and sold each watch for Rs. 250. What is the profit percentage gained?
questions.explanation: He bought 200 watches at Rs. 150/- = 200 * 150 = Rs. 30000/- He spent Rs. 10000 for the transportation = 30000 + 10000 = Rs. 40000/-. He sold the 200 watches at Rs. 250/- = 250 * 200 = Rs. 50000/- Profit = selling price - cost price = 50000 - 40000 = 10000 Percentage = 10000/40000 * 100 = 25%.

questions.question: Mohan marks up his items 20% above its cost price and gives a discount of 15% on it. In order to achieve higher profits, he cheats his customers by using weight of 950 grams instead of 1kg. What is his net profit/loss percentage?
questions.explanation: Let the cost price of 1 kg of item = Rs 1000a Marked price of the goods = Rs (1000a X 1.2) = Rs 1200a Selling price of the item = Rs (1200a X 0.85) = Rs 1020a According to the question Mohan uses weight of 950 grams instead of 1kg hence actual cost price of the item = Rs 950a Hence actual profit percentage = (1020a-950a)/950a X 100 = 7.4%

questions.question: In a college both boys and girls are studying. If 80% boys and 85% girls got passed, then a total of 216 students got failed. If 30% boys and 20% girls got failed, then a total of 313 students got failed then what percentage of the total students are girl students?
questions.explanation: Let us suppose the total number of girl and boys in the college are G and B respectively. According to the 1ˢᵗ condition; 0.2B + 0.15G = 216 .. (1) From 2ⁿᵈ information; 0.3B + 0.2G = 313 .. (2) Solving (1) and (2) we will get; B = 750 and G = 440 Therefore, total number of students in the college = (750 + 440) = 1190 Hence required percentage = (440/1190) X 100=37%

questions.question: What quantity of rice costing Rs. 18 per kg must be mixed with 27 kg of rice costing Rs. 14 per kg so that there may be a gain of 15% by selling the mixture at Rs. 19.32 per kg?
questions.explanation: SP = 19.32, profit = 15% Hence CP = 19.32/1.15 = Rs 16.8 Let x be the quantity of rice costing Rs. 18 per kg. Then, (18x + 14*27)/(x + 27) = 16.8 => x = 63 Quantity of 18/kg rice = 63 kg

questions.question: A train running at the speed of 72 km/h crosses a 250 m long platform in 23 seconds. How long will it take to cross a bridge which is 750 m long?
questions.explanation: Let the length of train = L m Distance covered to cross the platform = (L + 250) m According to question, (L + 250)/23 = 72 x 5/18 = 20 L + 250 = 460 or L = 210m Now the distance covered to cross the bridge = 750+210 = 960m Time taken = 960/20 = 48 seconds.

questions.question: What is the remainder when 37³ + 38³ + 39³ + 40³ is divisible by 77?
questions.explanation: 37³ + 38³ + 39³ + 40³ = (37³ + 40³) + (38³ + 39³) = (37 + 40) x + (38 + 39) Y. where X and Y are other factors = 77X + 77Y which is divisible by 77.

questions.question: ABCD is a rectangle of dimensions 4 units and 3 units. AEFC is a rectangle drawn in such a way that diagonal AC of the first rectangle is one side and the side opposite to it is touching the first rectangle at D. What is the ratio of the area of rectangle ABCD to that of AEFC?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/814376/image003.jpg) According to the figure above, AE² = CF² => AD² - DE² = CD² - DF² => 3² - x² = 4² - (5 - x)² => 9 - x² = 16 - (25 + x² - 10x) => 10x = 18 => x = 1.8 AE² = AD² - DE² => AE² = 3² - 1.8² = 9 - 3.24 = 5.76 => AE = 2.4 Ratio of area of rectangle ABCD to AEFC = (4 * 3):(5 * 2.4) = 1:1

questions.question: If 1 + sec θ + tan θ = P, then cos θ=
questions.explanation: sec θ + tan θ = P - 1 (i) Taking reciprocal, we have 1/(sec θ + tan θ) = 1/(P-1) => (sec θ - tan θ)/(sec² θ - tan² θ) = 1/(P-1) => sec θ - tan θ = 1/(P-1) (ii) (i) + (ii) => 2 sec θ = (P² - 2P + 2)/(P-1) => cos θ = 2(P-1)/(P² - 2P + 2)

questions.question: (sec² x-cosec²x) [ cos²x (sinx+cosx)_________cosec²x (sinx-cosx) + sin²x (sinx-cosx)_________sec² x(sinx+cosx) ] =
questions.explanation: (sec² x - cosec² x) = (sin² x - cos² x)/(sin² x cos² x) (cos² x(sinx+cosx)/(cosec² x(sinx-cosx))+(sin² x(sinx-cosx))/(sec² x(sinx+cosx))(sec² x-cosec² x) = (sinx + cosx)² + (sinx - cosx)² = 2(sin²x + cos²x) = 2

questions.question: Mr. X deposited certain amount in the bank at a certain rate for 2 years. Mr.X would have fetched Rs.24 more if the bank rate would have increased by 1%. What amount did Mr.X deposit?
questions.explanation: Let the amount deposited be P and the rate of interest be r%. (P X 2 X r)/100= (P X 2 X (r+1))/100 - 24 2 Pr=2Pr+2P-2400 2P= 2400 →P=Rs.1200

questions.question: If sinA+cosA / (sinA- cosA) = 4, Find sin⁴A - cos⁴A
questions.explanation: SinA+cosA /(sinA-cosA) = 4/1 Applying C & D sinA+cosA+sinA-cosA/sinA+cosA-sinA+cosA = 4+¼-1 => tanA = 5/3 => sec²A - tan²A = 1 => 25/9 + 1 = sec²A => 34/9 = sec²A => cos²A = 9/34 => sin²A = 1-cos²A = 1-9/34 => 25/34 Sin⁴A-cos⁴A = (sin²A-cos²A) (sin²A+cos²A) => 25/34-9/34 => 16/34

questions.question: Evaluate: 1 +1 1 +2 2 +3 1 + ⅘
In triangle ADE, AB = BC = CD, and BD = DE = BE. What is ∠DAE? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p587/image005.png)
questions.explanation: Let ∠A = 2θ. In triangle ABC, ∠ACB = 2θ. Again ∠ACB = ∠CBD + ∠CDB = 2∠CDB (∵ CB = CD). ∠CDB = θ. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p587/image006.png) Also, as triangle BDE is equilateral, ∠BDE = ∠E = 60°. In triangle ADE, 2θ + (θ+ 60) + 60 = 180 or 3θ + 120 = 180 or 2θ = 40.

questions.question: What is the difference between the number of electronic appliances produced among Television, Air Conditioners, Refrigerator and Washing Machine together and electronic appliances passed among Mobile, Laptop and Tablet together?
questions.explanation: Total number of electronic appliances produced among Television, Conditioners, Refrigerator and Washing Machine together = 2800 + 1800+ 2400 + 1500 = 8500 Total number of electronic appliances passed among Mobile, Laptop and Tablet together = 2200+1800+1000 = 5000 Required difference = 8500 - 5000 = 3500 Hence, Option 2 is correct.

questions.question: Find the average number of electronic appliances passed from all the appliances together?
questions.explanation: Average number of electronic appliances passed from all the appliances together = (2400+1600+2200+1400+2200+1800+1000)/7 = 1800 Hence, Option 1 is correct.

questions.question: Which one of the following electronic appliances has the maximum difference between appliances produced and passed?
questions.explanation: Television = 2800 - 2400 = 400 (maximum difference) Conditioners = 1800 - 1600 = 200 Refrigerator = 2400 - 2200 = 200 Washing Machine = 1500 - 1400 = 100 Mobile = 2400 - 2200 = 200 Laptop = 2000 - 1800 = 200 Tablet = 1200-1000 = 200 Hence, Option 4 is correct.

questions.question: Find the ratio between the number of electronic appliances passed among Television and the number of electronic appliances produced among Tablet?
questions.explanation: Ratio = 2400 : 1200 =2400/1200=2:1 Hence, Option 4 is correct.

questions.question: The average wage of 30 labors is Rs 140 .If 4 labors whose average wage was Rs. 90 leave the construction work, find the average wage of the remaining labours (rounded off to the nearest integer).
questions.explanation: Average wage = Rs. 140 Number of labors = 30 Thus, total units = 140 * 30 = 4200 Now the total number of labors who left the construction = 4 Their average wage = 4 * 90 = 360 So, the remaining wage = 4200 - 360 = 3840 Thus, the average wage of the remaining labors = 3840/ 26 = Rs. 148

questions.question: The ratio of length and breadth of a rectangle garden is 8: 5 respectively. If the breadth is 90 metre less than the length, what is the area of the garden?
questions.explanation: Let length and breadth of the garden be respectively 8x and 5x. So, breadth = length - 90 Thus, 5x = 8x - 90 Or, 3x = 90 Or, x = 30 So, length = 240 m and breadth = 150 m Thus, the area of the rectangle field = length X breadth = 150 X 240 = 36000 sq.m

questions.question: In the given figure O and O' are the centre of two circles and AP, BP and CP are the tangents then ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/sscimg/8023/image096.jpg)
questions.explanation: We know that tangents drown from an exterior point are equal in lengths. PA = PC ..(i) and PB = PC ..(ii) From eqs (i) and (ii) PA = PB = PC
